name: OECD AI Incidents and Related Terms Definitions
description: OECD's formal AI-terminology definitions from 'Defining AI Incidents and Related Terms' (OECD Artificial Intelligence Papers No. 16, May 2024, OECD DSTI/CDEP/AIGO(2023)10/FINAL). Defines 5 working terms — AI incident, serious AI incident, AI disaster, AI hazard, serious AI hazard — developed by the OECD.AI Expert Group on AI Incidents to support the OECD AI Incidents Monitor (AIM). OECD does not publish a standalone AI glossary page; these formal definitions are the closest thing.

license.notes: OECD publications are governed by the OECD Terms and Conditions (http://www.oecd.org/termsandconditions). Working papers like this AI paper are typically available under terms permitting copying and reproduction with attribution. © OECD 2024.

links.source_research_note: Note: the original metadata-recorded URL (https://oecd.ai/en/dashboards/policy-initiatives/glossary-of-terms-on-ai) is actually a Policy Observatory tracking-entry for Ukraine's AI glossary (Ukrainian-language, published by Ukraine's Ministry of Digital Transformation), not an OECD glossary. The OECD's actual AI terminology is in the Defining AI Incidents paper now linked above.
links.acquisition_blocker_resolved: The metadata-recorded source URL https://oecd.ai/en/dashboards/policy-initiatives/glossary-of-terms-on-ai no longer resolves to a glossary — it now serves an unrelated 'AI in Ukraine' policy initiative page. Browsing the OECD.AI Policy Observatory (oecd.ai/wonk, oecd.ai/ai-principles) and the OECD main site (oecd.org/en/topics/sub-issues/ai-principles) did not surface a current OECD AI glossary. OECD's AI definition lives inside their AI Principles document rather than as a standalone glossary page as of 2026-05-21. Re-investigate via OECD librarians or wait for a re-published glossary.

current_extraction.notes: Manual PDF download from oecd.org/en/publications. The PDF is gitignored (*.pdf); the markdown extraction + structured terms CSV are committed. 5 formal working definitions captured.

extraction_history.reason: Initial metadata stub created from glossary research sweep
